frameworks.name: level of mechanism
frameworks.one_line: Components of a mechanism are themselves decomposable into nested mechanisms.

frameworks.name: reductionism
frameworks.one_line: Reducing higher-level theory to lower-level substrate is premature given undeveloped theory.

frameworks.name: emergence (metaphysical)
frameworks.one_line: Strong emergence claims conflict with physicalism and are unhelpful for practice.

frameworks.name: model selection
frameworks.one_line: Adjudicating competing accounts against specified findings replaces vague plausibility appeals.

core_question: How should neuroscience handle levels of analysis, and what does 'biological plausibility' actually require?
key_assumptions: Mechanisms are hierarchically decomposable rather than tied to fixed ontological levels
Scientific goals are pragmatic, not metaphysical
Measures (behaviour, BOLD, single-unit) are not themselves levels of analysis
interpretations: Claims of 'biological plausibility' are incoherent under a level-of-mechanism view and largely vacuous
Model selection over explicit findings should replace reduction- and emergence-style framing
